prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, my dear! A CPU is like the brain of the computer—it’s the part that thinks and figures things out. When you click on a game or type a message, the CPU is the one doing all the quick little calculations to make it happen. It doesn’t remember things for long—that’s what the memory and hard drive are for—but it’s always busy making sure everything runs smoothly. Think of it like the conductor of an orchestra, telling all the other parts what to do and when. Even though I don’t quite get all the fancy tech talk, I know it’s mighty important!
